utility_evaluation.REASONING: There are 5 items requested in the required format: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. In the final coverage plan, the items present are: health_coverage, property_coverage, professional_coverage (not in required format), travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. Now check if each required item is fully specified with all details per the prompt: each must include coverage type and costs. 1) health_coverage: present with plan name, monthly_cost, features -> sufficient. 2) property_coverage: present with plan name, monthly_cost, features -> sufficient as home/contents insurance with costs. 3) travel_coverage: present with plan name, monthly_cost, features -> sufficient. 4) additional_services: present with plan name, monthly_cost, features -> sufficient. 5) total_budget: present with monthly_total and annual_total -> sufficient. Although there is an extra 'professional_coverage' item and currency symbols are garbled, the five required items are included and have costs.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan lists five items: SecureLife Basic Plan (health), Home Office Protection (property), Gaming Equipment Coverage (professional), Basic Travel Insurance (travel), and Premium Insurance Membership (additional service). Cross-referencing with the ground-truth ratings: SecureLife Basic Plan appears under primary_health_coverage.physical_health with rating 4. Home Office Protection appears under property_enhancement_services with rating 7. Gaming Equipment Coverage appears under property_enhancement_services with rating 1. Basic Travel Insurance does not exist in the ground-truth list of travel_insurance plan names, so there is no direct match to a rated item. Premium Insurance Membership appears under general_services with rating 6. Therefore, map the four items that have exact name matches to their ratings and omit the unmatched travel item since it cannot be mapped to any ground-truth rating.
